finding_results.notes: Converted the casualty beat into on-page witnessed harm (Im Byung-ho), including bodily action and sensory detail. Ina makes an explicit costed choice to deploy Hyejin's van, and later tally/bulletin passages now call back to the named victim rather than abstract counts.

Both ledger-heavy zones now include explicit Ina body-state/interior pressure and live interpersonal fallout tied to her choices (verification gatekeeping consequences in the morning block; seizure/rebuild fallout in the evening block).

Added an on-page confrontation with Captain Ryu and Joint Stability Taskforce in the post-Kwon section. Ina is forced into a binary decision (surrender records/device vs full building screening), chooses surrender, and immediately incurs operational loss.

summary: Revised chapter 15 now foregrounds embodied consequence over administrative abstraction, adds a direct opposing-force confrontation with an irreversible costed choice before the chapter-16 handoff call, and strengthens voice-consistent pressure dialogue while preserving continuity and boundary constraints.
